mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, it signals severe labor market slack and imminent recessionary pressures, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52 percent).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns during mid-cycle expansion phases (mean return <= 0.0 percent).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, it signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels indicate stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue generation, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 direction (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, it indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, triggering recession fears and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO drops below 90.0.
